# Sub PEQ filters
# miniDSP-style peaking filters plus normalized biquad coefficients
# Biquad convention: y[n] = b0*x[n] + b1*x[n-1] + b2*x[n-2] - a1*y[n-1] - a2*y[n-2]

Filter 1: ON PK Fc 58.128 Hz Gain -8.881 dB Q 7.4741
  biquad b0=0.99972841537 b1=-1.99913719747 b2=0.999423250115 a1=-1.99913719747 a2=0.999151665485
Filter 2: ON PK Fc 101.910 Hz Gain +0.274 dB Q 2.1222
  biquad b0=1.0000495852 b1=-1.9968667473 b2=0.996861581949 a1=-1.9968667473 a2=0.996911167149
Filter 3: ON PK Fc 80.367 Hz Gain -5.525 dB Q 7.9763
  biquad b0=0.999786808689 b1=-1.99906638075 b2=0.99930722694 a1=-1.99906638075 a2=0.999094035629
Filter 4: ON PK Fc 144.547 Hz Gain -4.948 dB Q 7.9373
  biquad b0=0.999656152682 b1=-1.99832712543 b2=0.99876040391 a1=-1.99832712543 a2=0.998416556593
Filter 5: ON PK Fc 44.164 Hz Gain +1.384 dB Q 3.8624
  biquad b0=1.00005965489 b1=-1.99930080372 b2=0.999249501159 a1=-1.99930080372 a2=0.999309156045
